title: Confirmatory stereochemical carry-vs-use target-map — FROZEN PROTOCOL
working_question: Across biological targets and chemical regimes, when does a molecular representation carry sufficient stereochemical information for an antisymmetric model to recover and generalize measured same-assay enantiomer potency differences?

representation.collision_floor_control: achiral radius 3 (useChirality=false) -> delta==0 -> equals zero-delta baseline exactly
representation.note: All collision rates reported as configuration-specific; r2->r3 shown to be a neighborhood-radius effect, not hashing (identical across 1k-8k bits and unhashed).

eligibility_criteria_DATA_ONLY.base: IC50, relation '=', nM, assay_type=B, confidence_score=9, data_validity NULL, potential_duplicate excluded, single-protein target, salt-stripped-then-re-paired, fully-defined-chiral true enantiomers (InChI /m trick), isotopes excluded

orientation: per-pair orientation-symmetrized (m0/m1 label not deployment-available); real metrics provably invariant, null correctly 0.5-centered

tie_handling.directional_metric: exclude exact Delta-y=0 ties from balanced directional accuracy; report tie prevalence separately

primary_statistic.name: normalized zero-delta skill score S_delta
primary_statistic.inference: empirical p from scaffold-cluster null; Benjamini-Hochberg FDR across targets

models.primary: symmetrized antisymmetric RF: dyhat(delta)=(h(delta)-h(-delta))/2, swap-augmented, hyperparams frozen from development cohort
models.second: regularized ridge on signed fingerprint difference delta (naturally antisymmetric; tests additive vs nonlinear recoverability)

splits.rule: both orientations + all multi-assay instances of a structural pair kept in-fold; effective N = pairs

multiple_testing: Benjamini-Hochberg FDR across targets on the PRIMARY (S_delta) p-values only; secondary-metric inference reported separately, not used to upgrade a target to positive
no_suppression: every qualifying target reported regardless of outcome

variability_secondary_only.estimate: sigma(Delta-y) ~= 0.45 log (robust global, from 2688 provenance-distinct nonidentical repeats; NOT called definitively independent)

preregistered_hypothesis.statement: Predictive stereochemical use is limited primarily by whether measured enantioselectivity exceeds experimental variability.
preregistered_hypothesis.per_target_ratio: R_t = median|Delta-y| / sigma(Delta-y),  sigma=0.45  (signal-to-variability; higher = more signal above noise)
preregistered_hypothesis.test: association of R_t with S_delta, Spearman, balanced directional accuracy across ALL qualifying targets
preregistered_hypothesis.status: preregistered hypothesis; NOT a headline until it survives the full cohort
